youtube.commentText: Ms. Bellows grew up without electricity or running water - given her embrace of Socialism, it would seem she wants the rest of the state to share her experience. As for our logger friend, apparently, he spends most of his time in the woods and hasn't heard of TrumpRX and the President's successful efforts to bring down the price of prescription medicines, or the efforts of RFK to remove harmful chemicals and additives from America's food supply. As for Mr. Shah, my question is 'How many more American citizens need to die at the hands of illegal immigrants?' I am saddened that anyone has lost their life, but the handful who have died because of unfortunate interactions with ICE pales in comparison to the many who have been murdered, raped, trafficked and abused by foreign nationals. It should be noted that, unlike those foreign nationals, ICE agents don't enter an engagement with a thirst to kill. Lastly, I respectfully submit that Mr. Jordan remove his shirt to see if there are any Nazi tatoos on HIS torso. While I have heard of a genocide being launched against Gaza, I have seen no evidence of it, apart from Hezbollah propaganda, despite the fact that every mainstream media outlet is slavering for it and would blow it out of all proportion if it was being implemented. While three of the candidates here seem to be Mamdani wanna-bes, Jordan seems the least capable (he couldn't even memorize his opening statement?!), and, therefore, will be most appealing to the Democrat base in Maine. Of the four here, Mr. Jackson seems the least radical and, therefore, his chances are slim to none among the socialist wing of his party.

social.postText: ETN AMERICA BREAKING: Shenna Bellows has withdrawn from the Maine Senate snap primary contest, according to an email she sent supporters.

Shenna Bellows withdraws from the senate race and throws her support to defeating Susan Collins in Nov.

#MEpolitics #MEsen #Maine #WorkingClassPower

Winning the hearts of Graham Platner's apologists in this snap primary is not the same as winning the election. 

Troy Jackson is a good person, but he tied himself to an evil clown. Susan Collins will hammer him on that.

Shenna Bellows is a much better choice for the Democratic nominee.

I was not on the official list handed out by the Bellows team yesterday in Hancock, but I was on an unofficial handwritten list that circulated.

I didn’t state my preference for Shenna before hand, but my wife and I have personal connections, which is why my name was included on the unofficial list

I am really hoping to see Shenna Bellows be our candidate.
I have way too many unanswered questions about Troy & his alleged bias against Women's Rights.
Shenna has already shown us she can & will stand up to trump. I have no worries either that she will protect Women's Rights.

It's worth noting that several on Troy Jackson's slate for Kennebec County also were on Shenna Bellows' slate. I think there were other overlaps as well. I don't think today's results are as much of a clean sweep as his team is proclaiming, but we will see next week.

I’m excited about Troy Jackson. He’s got great politics just like Shenna Bellows does, and he’s got a great amount of support which is really heartening to see. If either he or Shenna get in I’m excited, because we could count on them to do the right thing in office
Our Revolun endorsed Troy Jackson. I don’t know why you bring up Shenna Bellows.

mainemorningstar.com/2025/03/26/m...

Hoping @shennabellows.bsky.social gets the Dem. nomination so we can beat Sen. Collins. 

#MainePolitics  #GoodLuckShenna

news.title: Troy Jackson closes in on Maine’s Democratic Senate nod after dominant caucus day
news.description: Former Maine Senate President Troy Jackson fell just 12 delegates short of clinching the race during Saturday's unprecedented county caucuses.

youtube.commentText: Ms. Bellows grew up without electricity or running water - given her embrace of Socialism, it would seem she wants the rest of the state to share her experience. As for our logger friend, apparently, he spends most of his time in the woods and hasn't heard of TrumpRX and the President's successful efforts to bring down the price of prescription medicines, or the efforts of RFK to remove harmful chemicals and additives from America's food supply. As for Mr. Shah, my question is 'How many more American citizens need to die at the hands of illegal immigrants?' I am saddened that anyone has lost their life, but the handful who have died because of unfortunate interactions with ICE pales in comparison to the many who have been murdered, raped, trafficked and abused by foreign nationals. It should be noted that, unlike those foreign nationals, ICE agents don't enter an engagement with a thirst to kill. Lastly, I respectfully submit that Mr. Jordan remove his shirt to see if there are any Nazi tatoos on HIS torso. While I have heard of a genocide being launched against Gaza, I have seen no evidence of it, apart from Hezbollah propaganda, despite the fact that every mainstream media outlet is slavering for it and would blow it out of all proportion if it was being implemented. While three of the candidates here seem to be Mamdani wanna-bes, Jordan seems the least capable (he couldn't even memorize his opening statement?!), and, therefore, will be most appealing to the Democrat base in Maine. Of the four here, Mr. Jackson seems the least radical and, therefore, his chances are slim to none among the socialist wing of his party.
